item.title: Police investigate death of Beqa man after alleged Police brutality during drug raid
item.description: The Police Criminal Investigations Department is investigating the death of a Dakuni, Beqa man after alleged Police brutality during a drug raid. When asked by fijivillage News, Police confirmed the post mortem is scheduled for Monday to determine the cause of death.
item.content:encoded:
The Police Criminal Investigations Department is investigating the death of a Dakuni, Beqa man after alleged Police brutality during a drug raid.

When asked by fijivillage News, Police confirmed the post mortem is scheduled for Monday to determine the cause of death.

The man's wife, Anne Vakararawa is calling for justice as investigations into the incident continue.

We had earlier reported that some police officers and a military officer were allegedly involved in an incident that left Iveri Tuimasi injured in Dakuni Village, Beqa.

The victim’s wife is alleging unnecessary and excessive use of force during the police operation in March.

Vakararawa says the officers discovered two marijuana plants during the raid.

item.title: fijivillage Straight Talk live at 7pm Wednesday on HIV cases and the work underway
item.description: A special fijivillage Straight Talk With Vijay Narayan focusing on HIV and the work of the National HIV Outbreak and Cluster Response Taskforce, will be held live at 7pm next Wednesday from Fiji Showcase 2026 at the Vodafone Arena. The guests on the show will be led by the Chair of the National HIV Outbreak and Cluster Response Taskforce, Doctor Jason Mitchell.
item.content:encoded:
A special fijivillage Straight Talk With Vijay Narayan focusing on HIV and the work of the National HIV Outbreak and Cluster Response Taskforce, will be held live at 7pm next Wednesday from Fiji Showcase 2026 at the Vodafone Arena.

The guests on the show will be led by the Chair of the National HIV Outbreak and Cluster Response Taskforce, Doctor Jason Mitchell.

We have a town-hall set up for this live show where you can also be part of the audience to participate on the show, and ask questions that you will have to send in by 8am next Tuesday.

You can send your questions focusing on the HIV statistics in Fiji, where we are at in terms of community-led health outreach, and the clinical prevention rollouts underway.

You can either send in your questions to vijaynarayan@fm96.com.fj or refer to our fijivillage social media pages to put in your questions.

If your question is selected to be asked on the show, you have to be a Fiji Showcase 2026 patron on Wednesday evening, to ask the question in person.

You can watch the show on our facebook page, YouTube channel, TikTok and X account, fijivillage at 7pm next Wednesday.

You can also catch the podcast, fijivillage Straight Talk With Vijay Narayan on Spotify and Apple Podcasts.

item.title: Jo Nata says 2000 coup was based on lies and they were misguided fools
Residents raise concerns over prolonged power outage in Naduru, Nausori
item.description: Residents of Prakash Feeder Road in Naduru, Nausori are calling on Energy Fiji Limited to urgently restore power after an extended outage disrupted the lives of around 40 households. One resident told fijivillage News that power was switched off at around 6pm on Thursday and was restored at about 1pm yesterday before being cut off again at around 6pm.
item.content:encoded:
Residents of Prakash Feeder Road in Naduru, Nausori are calling on Energy Fiji Limited to urgently restore power after an extended outage disrupted the lives of around 40 households.

One resident told fijivillage News that power was switched off at around 6pm on Thursday and was restored at about 1pm yesterday before being cut off again at around 6pm.

She says she contacted EFL's toll-free number and was informed that the outage was linked to the fuel crisis and that repair crews do not work after 5pm or on weekends.

She claims they were told power may not be restored until Monday.

She adds that many families in the area are working parents who rely on weekends to complete household chores.

HINDI INSERT: Resident raises concerns over power cut June 20

The resident is calling on EFL to maintain emergency response teams to deal with such situations and restore power as quickly as possible.

Meanwhile, in response to our questions, EFL has clarified that the outage is most likely due to a fault and not the result of the fuel crisis.

The company says restoration work on faults is carried out on the next working day.

item.title: PM says we will have elections as Seruiratu raises concerns about any attempted delay
item.description: Vijay Narayan , Navitalai Naivalurua · 20/06/2026 Prime Minister, Sitiveni Rabuka stresses that the Constitution and the parliamentary and electoral systems are clear - we will have elections.
item.content:encoded:
Vijay Narayan , Navitalai Naivalurua · 20/06/2026

Prime Minister, Sitiveni Rabuka stresses that the Constitution and the parliamentary and electoral systems are clear - we will have elections.

Rabuka has made this clear to fijivillage News after Leader of the Opposition, Inia Seruiratu raised concerns about R abuka earlier saying that the Government is considering whether the next election should proceed according to the constitutional timetable.

When questioned by us, Rabuka says the g overnment tried for a parliamentary vote, and the Opposition successfully used their imposed conditions to block it, so the Coalition he confidently leads will adopt the only option open - to continue to run down the clock on current provisions while also following due processes to amend the various laws that need to be changed.

When asked on whether these suggestions of the government trying to delay the elections based on Constitution changes is concerning him and what is his message to the people, Rabuka says no, co nstituonal changes are parliamentary processes.

He says governments work in accordance with Constitutions until the Constitution is changed.

The Prime Minister says the People’s Alliance Party came in with the mandate from their voters to change the Constitution and its electoral processes.

He says they are pursuing those, and whether they finish them or not, they are bound to turn the mandate back to the people to elect their representatives according to the existing laws.

Rabuka says he is ready to go to elections when the laws dictate either to go the full term or to call for early elections according to law.

Leader of the Opposition Inia Seruiratu has said in a statement that any attempt to delay the next general election risks creating a constitutional crisis and undermining public confidence in Fiji's democratic process.

Seruiratu says Fiji is only months away from the dissolution of Parliament.

He says it is unrealistic to expect major constitutional amendments, electoral reforms, public consultations, parliamentary processes and any potential referendum to be completed in a credible and democratic manner before the next election.

Seruiratu says the Coalition Government has had almost four years to pursue constitutional and electoral reforms and, if these were genuine priorities, they should have been advanced much earlier.

He says the people of Fiji should not be asked to accept uncertainty because of the Government's failure to manage its timetable.

The Opposition Leader has also expressed concern that the current constitutional review process is being rushed, arguing that many ordinary Fijians have not had a meaningful opportunity to participate in discussions about the country's Constitution.

Seruiratu says any constitutional changes must be undertaken carefully and with genuine public participation, not rushed to satisfy political timelines.

He also claims the Prime Minister appears more focused on securing his legacy than addressing issues affecting ordinary Fijians, including the rising cost of living, healthcare, crime, infrastructure and economic uncertainty.

Seruiratu notes that the Electoral Commission has already indicated its readiness to conduct the next general election and says the Government should respect the constitutional timetable and allow Fijians to exercise their democratic right to choose their next government.

item.title: International entertainer Aussie Bela ready to rock the 2026 Fiji Showcase
item.description: International entertainers Duo AussieBela will rock the 2026 Fiji Showcase with acrobatic moves, illusions, and many other tricks to keep the crowd entertained. Speaking to fijivillage News, they say the hospitality they received from the people and the organisers has been overwhelming, and they are excited to perform.
item.content:encoded:
International entertainers Duo AussieBela will rock the 2026 Fiji Showcase with acrobatic moves, illusions, and many other tricks to keep the crowd entertained.

Speaking to fijivillage News, they say the hospitality they received from the people and the organisers has been overwhelming, and they are excited to perform.

AussieBela says they have performed around the world in alot of shows and on cruise ships as well, and they are very passionate about their show.

Insert AussieBela on Fiji tour 19th June

item.title: AussieBela, Tauvu Tribute, Savu ni Delai Lomai and Piva light up Day 1 of Fiji Showcase 2026
item.description: Spectacular performances from international acrobatic duo AussieBela, Tauvu Tribute and Savu ni Delai Lomai brought Day One of Fij

item.title: Family support drives Mereani Folau Kacilala to Police Force success
item.description: Mother of three, Mereani Folau Kacilala, was overcome with emotion after passing out from the Fiji Police Force. Kacilala told fijivillage News that her children were her greatest source of motivation and strength throughout the demanding 19-week training programme.
item.content:encoded:
Mother of three, Mereani Folau Kacilala, was overcome with emotion after passing out from the Fiji Police Force.

Kacilala told fijivillage News that her children were her greatest source of motivation and strength throughout the demanding 19-week training programme.

Reflecting on her journey, she expressed gratitude for the unwavering support and encouragement of her family, particularly her children, who stood by her throughout the challenging yet rewarding experience.

Her achievement marks a proud milestone for her family, showcasing determination, resilience, and the importance of family support in achieving one's goals.

She said that through her faith in God and self-determination, she was able to successfully complete the training.

Kacilala is no stranger to rigorous training, having played netball for many years and previously being part of the extended training squad for the Fiji Pearls.

Her mother and three children were present at the Nasova Grounds to celebrate her achievement, making the occasion even more special.

Kacilala said she is now ready to serve the nation and its people with pride and commitment.

Prime Minister Sitiveni Rabuka, who was the chief guest at the ceremony, reminded the officers to carry out their duties in accordance with police ethics and professional standards.

He also urged the new officers to uphold the standards of the force and lead by example as law-abiding citizens.

More than 150 police officers took part in the pass-out parade.

item.title: Villagers in Lau to benefit from new jobs and income opportunities
item.description: Villagers in Lakeba and the wider Lau Group are expected to benefit from new jobs and income opportunities following the handover
